failure_surface: Evaluation returned 0 (failed), indicating the output file did not meet expected criteria despite successful code execution
root_cause: Agent ignored the explicit instruction requiring VBA code and instead provided Python/openpyxl code. Additionally, the direct value assignment via openpyxl may have overwritten formulas in column Q that needed to remain intact, and case-sensitive string comparisons in Python do not match Excel's case-insensitive behavior.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: The agent should either provide VBA code when explicitly requested by the user, OR if using Python, ensure case-insensitive string matching (e.g., .lower() comparison) and verify no formulas exist in target cells before overwriting values
evidence: Instruction explicitly states 'VBA is preferred as it is already used for data import and cleanup'
Solution uses pandas/openpyxl Python code instead of VBA macro
Using ws.cell(row=row, column=17, value='Volkswagen') directly overwrites any existing formula in that cell
String comparisons use exact match (==) without case normalization
Output saved successfully but test_case_results shows 0 (complete failure)

memory_items.title: Respect Explicit Tool Requirements
memory_items.lesson: When instructions explicitly specify a particular tool (VBA, Python, manual steps), honor that requirement even if alternative methods could achieve similar results
memory_items.generalization: User tool preferences often reflect environment constraints, integration needs, or compliance requirements that override technical equivalence

memory_items.title: Preserve Formulas When Writing Values
memory_items.lesson: Direct cell value assignment in openpyxl overwrites any existing formula; check if target cells contain formulas before overwriting
memory_items.generalization: Use openpyxl.formula property to inspect cells before applying direct value assignments, especially in business spreadsheets where calculated fields are common

memory_items.title: Case-Insensitive String Matching for Excel Data
memory_items.lesson: Excel comparisons are case-insensitive while Python is case-sensitive; normalize strings (.lower()) when comparing text values extracted from spreadsheets
memory_items.generalization: Always consider case sensitivity differences between spreadsheet engines and programming languages when implementing conditional logic on text columns

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### 7. Honor Explicit Tool Requirements
- User-specified tools (VBA, Python scripts, manual operations) often indicate environmental constraints or integration workflows.
- Do not substitute alternative tools unless explicitly permitted by the instructions.

### 8. Formula Integrity Before Value Assignment
- Before writing static values to cells, check if they contain existing formulas using `cell.value == cell.data_type` inspection.
- Overwriting formulas with static values permanently breaks calculation chains in downstream dependent cells.

### 9. Cross-Language Case Sensitivity Handling
- Spreadsheet engines (Excel, Google Sheets) perform case-insensitive text matching.
- Programming languages (Python) require case-insensitive comparisons using `.lower()` or `.casefold()` when replicating Excel logic.
